brand_home.title: {brandName} is ready to arrange.
brand_home.body: I’ll turn the confirmed foundation into a calm home for logos, colors, type, reference files, downloads, and usage notes.

cards.title: Logo Library
cards.body: Primary logos, icon marks, wordmarks, and alternates live together instead of being scattered across folders.

cards.title: Color System
cards.body: The detected palette becomes a usable system with primary, supporting, background, and text roles.

cards.title: Typography
cards.body: Fonts and type guesses are documented clearly, even when the original files are incomplete.

cards.title: Downloads
cards.body: The final package stays available for export once the system is approved.
cards.meta: ZIP and builder handoff stay in Founder Mode

logos.title: Start with the identity system.
logos.body: This page keeps the logo family focused: primary logo, icon mark, wordmark, and supporting alternates.

cards.title: Primary Logo
cards.body: The lead identity asset for covers, headers, and key brand moments.

cards.title: Icon Mark
cards.body: The small-space mark for profiles, favicons, social avatars, and app-like usage.

cards.title: Wordmark
cards.body: The type-led version of the identity, used where clarity matters most.

cards.title: Alternates
cards.body: Secondary crops, lockups, and supporting files stay organized below the core system.

colors.title: Build the palette into a system.
colors.body: Detected colors become roles the customer can understand and reuse — not just random swatches.

cards.title: Primary Color
cards.body: The color that carries the strongest brand recognition.

cards.title: Supporting Colors
cards.body: Secondary colors for layouts, sections, and visual rhythm.

cards.title: Backgrounds
cards.body: Light and dark environments prepared for practical use.

cards.title: Usage Notes
cards.body: Simple direction for when colors should and should not be used.

assets.title: Separate visuals from references.
assets.body: This page explains what is usable brand artwork versus what should be treated as reference material.

cards.title: Visual Assets
cards.body: Logos, marks, photography, screenshots, and graphics ready for placement.

cards.title: Reference Files
cards.body: Decks, PDFs, notes, and documents that inform the system without becoming visual assets.

cards.title: Guidelines
cards.body: Any existing rules or brand standards kept as source material.

cards.title: Needs Attention
cards.body: Anything missing, low-resolution, duplicated, or unclear can be flagged here.

downloads.title: Package the system when it is ready.
downloads.body: Downloads are the final handoff layer. Keep the customer view calm and expose the technical controls only when needed.

cards.title: Logo Package
cards.body: Clean originals and export-ready variants prepared for delivery.

cards.title: Brand Notes
cards.body: Color, typography, and usage recommendations in one place.

cards.title: Reference Archive
cards.body: Supporting files preserved separately from the visual system.

cards.title: Final Handoff
cards.body: Everything can be zipped, shared, or pushed into the builder from Founder Mode.
